expectations.text: Response provides a TypeScript code block using server.registerTool or equivalent MCP registration
expectations.evidence: Uses a plain JSON object literal format (not server.registerTool). No TypeScript SDK registration shown — just a raw tool config object.

expectations.text: Tool description explains what the tool *returns* not just what it does
expectations.evidence: Description says 'Returns an array of pull request objects' and lists use cases for finding PRs

expectations.text: Input schema uses Zod with .describe() annotations on at least 2 fields
expectations.evidence: Uses raw JSON Schema format (type: 'string', description: '...') not Zod. No Zod imports or .describe() chains.

expectations.text: Response includes readOnlyHint: true in the annotations
expectations.evidence: annotations: { readOnlyHint: true, destructiveHint: false, idempotentHint: true, openWorldHint: true }

expectations.text: Response includes pagination support (per_page or page parameter) with a max constraint
expectations.evidence: per_page: { type: 'integer', minimum: 1, maximum: 100, default: 30 } and page parameter included

expectations.text: Response returns both content (text) and structuredContent in the tool handler
expectations.evidence: No tool handler/implementation shown. Response only provides the tool definition schema, not the async handler that returns content + structuredContent.
